summary: Tested DSP File Importer (Iteration 26). The modal, dropzone, file input, two-column preview, auto-tag badges, applied banner, read-only safety, bad-file error and InputStrip name/category reflection all work correctly. However, a critical parser bug means OUTPUT channel names are never updated when the test file contains fewer than 32+32 records — all parsed names get assigned to inputs and the outputs slice is empty.

ui_bugs.issue: Hard-coded split `inputs=all.slice(0,32); outputs=all.slice(32,64);` means that any file with <32 detected name records assigns ALL names to inputs and 0 to outputs. Step 6 verified state.outputs[0].name remained the default 'OUT 1' (never overwritten by 'SUB L'). The final Channels-view screenshot shows all 8 imported names (KICK..FRONT R) on input strips and no output rename. Recommend either (a) detect the actual input/output boundary by record count / format marker, or (b) when totalFound is small, split 50/50 between inputs and outputs, or (c) expose a manual offset slider in the modal.

Because of the above parser bug, with the spec's 4+4 test file the preview UI labels all 8 names as 'Inputs' and shows 0 outputs in the right column, contradicting the spec requirement that the first 4 → inputs and next 4 → outputs.

integration_issues.issue: state.outputs[*].name and state.outputs[*].category are not patched for short files, so downstream ChannelStrip / print map / EQ titles never reflect imported output names. The spec's apply test (SUB L/R, FRONT L/R) cannot succeed without a real 64-record file.

action_items: Fix parseDantDspNames so it correctly maps names to outputs when the total record count is < 64 (e.g., split at totalFound/2 when totalFound < 64, or expose an Inputs/Outputs split control in the modal).
After fix, re-run the importer test with a synthetic 4+4 file and assert state.outputs[0..3].name matches ['SUB L','SUB R','FRONT L','FRONT R'] and state.outputs[0].category === 'bass'.
critical_code_review_comments: dspBinaryImporter.js: rigid 32-input / 32-output assumption is fragile. The real DSP exports we can detect have a small sample size; the heuristic should be data-driven (split at the actual count) rather than positional.
dspBinaryImporter.js NAME_TRIM_OFFSET (line 17) is declared but never used — dead code.
guessCategory(name) is invoked twice per row inside the preview map (lines 131-134, 147-149) — memoise to avoid redundant regex execution on every render.

DspImportModal.handleFile does not reset `parsed` state when an invalid file replaces a previously valid one — visually the old preview can briefly remain visible if `parsed` is set, but it is set to null in the error branch (OK). Still consider clearing on entry.
DspImportModal does not show file size or a progress indicator; large .dsp files would hang the UI silently.
seed_data_creation: Synthetic 254-byte .dsp file constructed in-memory (4 input + 4 output records with magic 0xB0E0E3 + 6 ASCII spaces + name + null padding + terminator 0xE840ED). Passed to dsp-import-input via page.set_input_files buffer payload.
context_for_next_testing_agent: PASSED (8/10 acceptance items): open-import button render, modal open + disabled apply, file ingestion (totalFound='8'), preview names visible, auto-tag category badges (drum/bass/vox/mic all rendered), apply success message + button disable, read-only safety (apply disabled when locked), bad-file error message + apply stays disabled, names visible in Channels view, category-tag elements show Drum/Drum/Bass/Vox/Bass/Bass/Mic/Mic on InputStrips. FAILED (2/10): (1) state.outputs[0..3].name not updated — see backend issue; verify state.outputs[0].name === 'SUB L' after apply with the synthetic 4+4 file. (2) regression check tried to find cal-detect on the channels tab — it's only rendered inside MetersView, so this isn't an app bug, just a test-script note. To rebuild the synthetic file: MAGIC=bytes([0xB0,0xE0,0xE3]); TERM=bytes([0xE8,0x40,0xED]); for each name: MAGIC + b'      ' + name.encode() + b'\x00'*4 + TERM + b'\x00'*8.
rca of the issue: Root cause: /app/frontend/src/lib/dspBinaryImporter.js lines 51-52 hard-code `inputs = all.slice(0, 32); outputs = all.slice(32, 64);`. For the spec-provided 4+4 synthetic file totalFound=8, so all.slice(32,64) returns [] and outputs are silently ignored. Apply() then loops state.outputs but every parsed.outputs[idx] is undefined → no patch. Reproduction: import any file with <32 valid records; observe state.outputs untouched. Mitigation: replace the hard-coded slice with a half-split when totalFound < 64 (e.g., const half = Math.ceil(totalFound/2); inputs = all.slice(0, half); outputs = all.slice(half, totalFound);) OR detect a sentinel byte between the input-block and output-block. The latter is preferable for real 32+32 production files but the former unblocks the spec'd test.
